pages.raw_text:
First announcement of another
S150,000 office building for North
Monroe street, to be located on the
southwest corner of Maxwell ave-
niue. was made yesterday by j. w.
Greene. the real estate man and
capitalist, who, more than anyone
else in Spokane, has been a consist
ent booster for many years of that
part of the city north of the river.


This time Mr.. Greene, who has
already invested several hundred
thousands of dollars in apartment
houses and office structures on the
north side, will build a strictly mod
ern steel frame building, resting on
concrete piers. 1t will be five Stor-
les high, the ground floor to be de-
voted to stores and the upper stor-
les to offices, 10 on floor Plans
for the building will be completed
at the office of Cutter & Malmgren,
architects, by May /. and work will
probably be started, says Mr..
Greene, shortly afterward.


The Monroe office building, being
erected on the southwest corner of
Monroe street and Indiana avenue
by Mr. Greene, is now almost fin
ished, the interior finishing being
practically all that remains. This
building is costing Mr.. Greene
about $4,000, or $4,000 more than
he originally intended to put into
the4 structure. 1t is the second
monument Mr.. Greene has built to
his faith in the future of the north
side. He sold his apartment house,
erected not long ago, recently.


TO BE SPLENDID, MODERN


STRUCTURE.


The Greene building" as the
new office structure now being
planned will be called, will be Of
brick, faced on the exterior Of the
first story with terra Gotta and with
similar trimmings over windows
and elsewhere on the outside.
There will be two large stores
fronting on North Monroe street.
The building will extend 100 feet on
Monroe street and the stores will
be about 10 feet wide. There will
be II feet frontage on Maxwell ave-
nue.. Costing $4,000 more than Mr..
